db_instance.description: RDS custom iam instance profile
db_instance.name: custom_iam_instance_profile

db_instance.description: Storage throughput value for the DB instance. This setting applies only to the `gp3` storage type. See `notes` for limitations regarding this variable for `gp3`
db_instance.name: storage_throughput

db_instance.description: The ARN for the KMS encryption key. If creating an encrypted replica, set this to the destination KMS ARN. If storage_encrypted is set to true and kms_key_id is not specified the default KMS key created in your account will be used
db_instance.name: kms_key_id

db_instance.description:
The key ARN, key ID, alias ARN or alias name for the KMS key to encrypt the master user password secret in Secrets Manager.
  If not specified, the default KMS key for your Amazon Web Services account is used.
db_instance.name: master_user_secret_kms_key_id

db_instance.description: The ARN for the KMS key to encrypt Performance Insights data.
db_instance.name: performance_insights_kms_key_id

db_instance.description: Specifies the value for Storage Autoscaling
db_instance.name: max_allocated_storage

db_instance.description: Specifies whether to remove automated backups immediately after the DB instance is deleted
db_instance.name: delete_automated_backups

db_instance.description: The type of network stack
db_instance.name: network_type

db_instance.description: Indicates that minor engine upgrades will be applied automatically to the DB instance during the maintenance window
db_instance.name: auto_minor_version_upgrade

db_instance.description: Region where this resource will be managed. Defaults to the Region set in the provider configuration
db_instance.name: region

db_instance.description: Specifies that this resource is a Replicate database, and to use this value as the source database. This correlates to the identifier of another Amazon RDS Database to replicate.
db_instance.name: replicate_source_db

db_instance.description: (Required if domain is provided) The name of the IAM role to be used when making API calls to the Directory Service
db_instance.name: domain_iam_role_name

db_instance.description: Set to true to allow RDS to manage the master user password in Secrets Manager. Cannot be set if password is provided
db_instance.name: manage_master_user_password

db_instance.description: The database can't be deleted when this value is set to true.
db_instance.name: deletion_protection

db_instance.description: Whether to manage the master user password rotation. By default, false on creation, rotation is managed by RDS. There is not currently a way to disable this on initial creation even when set to false. Setting this value to false after previously having been set to true will disable automatic rotation.
db_instance.name: manage_master_user_password_rotation

db_instance.description: Whether to create this resource or not?
db_instance.name: create

db_instance.description: The allocated storage in gigabytes
db_instance.name: allocated_storage

db_instance.description: Determines whether a final DB snapshot is created before the DB instance is deleted. If true is specified, no DBSnapshot is created. If false is specified, a DB snapshot is created before the DB instance is deleted
db_instance.name: skip_final_snapshot

db_instance.description: Name of DB subnet group. DB instance will be created in the VPC associated with the DB subnet group. If unspecified, will be created in the default VPC
db_instance.name: db_subnet_group_name

db_instance.description: The mode of Database Insights that is enabled for the instance. Valid values: standard, advanced
db_instance.name: database_insights_mode

db_instance.description: The days to retain backups for
db_instance.name: backup_retention_period

db_instance.description: Restore to a point in time (MySQL is NOT supported)
db_instance.name: restore_to_point_in_time
db_instance.type:
object({
    restore_time                             = optional(string)
    source_db_instance_automated_backups_arn = optional(string)
    source_db_instance_identifier            = optional(string)
    source_dbi_resource_id                   = optional(string)
    use_latest_restorable_time               = optional(bool)
  })

db_instance.description: Whether to upgrade the storage file system configuration on the read replica. Can only be set with replicate_source_db.
db_instance.name: upgrade_storage_config

db_instance.description: License model information for this DB instance. Optional, but required for some DB engines, i.e. Oracle SE1
db_instance.name: license_model

db_instance.description: Used together with password_wo to trigger an update. Increment this value when an update to password_wo is required.
db_instance.name: password_wo_version

db_instance.description: Name of the IAM role which will be created when create_monitoring_role is enabled.
db_instance.name: monitoring_role_name

db_instance.description: Set to true if you do not wish the log group (and any logs it may contain) to be deleted at destroy time, and instead just remove the log group from the Terraform state
db_instance.name: cloudwatch_log_group_skip_destroy

db_instance.description: Additional tags for the CloudWatch log group(s)
db_instance.name: cloudwatch_log_group_tags

db_instance.description: Specifies whether to rotate the secret immediately or wait until the next scheduled rotation window.
db_instance.name: master_user_password_rotate_immediately

db_instance.description: Specifies whether or mappings of AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) accounts to database accounts is enabled
db_instance.name: iam_database_authentication_enabled

db_instance.description: The life cycle type for this DB instance. This setting applies only to RDS for MySQL and RDS for PostgreSQL. Valid values are `open-source-rds-extended-support`, `open-source-rds-extended-support-disabled`. Default value is `open-source-rds-extended-support`.
db_instance.name: engine_lifecycle_support

db_instance.description: On delete, copy all Instance tags to the final snapshot
db_instance.name: copy_tags_to_snapshot

db_instance.description: Enables low-downtime updates using RDS Blue/Green deployments.
db_instance.name: blue_green_update

db_instance.description: Restore from a Percona Xtrabackup in S3 (only MySQL is supported)
db_instance.name: s3_import

db_instance.description: Specifies whether the replica is in either mounted or open-read-only mode. This attribute is only supported by Oracle instances. Oracle replicas operate in open-read-only mode unless otherwise specified
db_instance.name: replica_mode

db_instance.description: The DB name to create. If omitted, no database is created initially
db_instance.name: db_name

db_instance.description: The ARN of the KMS Key to use when encrypting log data
db_instance.name: cloudwatch_log_group_kms_key_id

db_instance.description: The Availability Zone of the RDS instance
db_instance.name: availability_zone

db_instance.description: The interval, in seconds, between points when Enhanced Monitoring metrics are collected for the DB instance. To disable collecting Enhanced Monitoring metrics, specify 0. The default is 0. Valid Values: 0, 1, 5, 10, 15, 30, 60.
db_instance.name: monitoring_interval

db_instance.description: The character set name to use for DB encoding in Oracle instances. This can't be changed. See Oracle Character Sets Supported in Amazon RDS and Collations and Character Sets for Microsoft SQL Server for more information. This can only be set on creation.
db_instance.name: character_set_name

db_instance.description: (Optional, but required if domain_fqdn is provided) The self managed Active Directory organizational unit for your DB instance to join. Conflicts with domain and domain_iam_role_name.
db_instance.name: domain_ou

db_instance.description: The engine version to use
db_instance.name: engine_version

db_instance.description: Bool to control if instance is publicly accessible
db_instance.name: publicly_accessible

db_instance.description: The window to perform maintenance in. Syntax: 'ddd:hh24:mi-ddd:hh24:mi'. Eg: 'Mon:00:00-Mon:03:00'
db_instance.name: maintenance_window

db_instance.description: (Optional, but required if domain_fqdn is provided) The ARN for the Secrets Manager secret with the self managed Active Directory credentials for the user joining the domain. Conflicts with domain and domain_iam_role_name.
db_instance.name: domain_auth_secret_arn

db_instance.description: The amount of provisioned IOPS. Setting this implies a storage_type of 'io1' or `gp3`. See `notes` for limitations regarding this variable for `gp3`
db_instance.name: iops

db_instance.description: A map of additional tags for the DB instance
db_instance.name: db_instance_tags

db_instance.description: Specified the log class of the log group. Possible values are: STANDARD or INFREQUENT_ACCESS
db_instance.name: cloudwatch_log_group_class

db_instance.description: Specifies whether any database modifications are applied immediately, or during the next maintenance window
db_instance.name: apply_immediately

db_instance.description: Specifies whether the DB instance is encrypted
db_instance.name: storage_encrypted

db_instance.description: List of VPC security groups to associate
db_instance.name: vpc_security_group_ids

db_instance.description: Name of the DB parameter group to associate
db_instance.name: parameter_group_name

db_instance.description: Specifies if the RDS instance is multi-AZ
db_instance.name: multi_az

db_instance.description: List of log types to enable for exporting to CloudWatch logs. If omitted, no logs will be exported. Valid values (depending on engine): alert, audit, error, general, listener, slowquery, trace, postgresql (PostgreSQL), upgrade (PostgreSQL).
db_instance.name: enabled_cloudwatch_logs_exports

db_instance.description: Use a dedicated log volume (DLV) for the DB instance. Requires Provisioned IOPS.
db_instance.name: dedicated_log_volume

db_instance.description: The number of days to retain CloudWatch logs for the DB instance
db_instance.name: cloudwatch_log_group_retention_in_days

db_instance.description: The fully qualified domain name (FQDN) of the self managed Active Directory domain. Conflicts with domain and domain_iam_role_name.
db_instance.name: domain_fqdn

db_instance.description: ARN of the policy that is used to set the permissions boundary for the monitoring IAM role
db_instance.name: monitoring_role_permissions_boundary

db_instance.description: The length of the rotation window in hours. For example, 3h for a three hour window.
db_instance.name: master_user_password_rotation_duration

db_instance.description: One of 'standard' (magnetic), 'gp2' (general purpose SSD), 'gp3' (new generation of general purpose SSD), or 'io1' (provisioned IOPS SSD). The default is 'io1' if iops is specified, 'gp2' if not. If you specify 'io1' or 'gp3' , you must also include a value for the 'iops' parameter
db_instance.name: storage_type

db_instance.description: Write-Only required unless `manage_master_user_password` is set to `true`, `snapshot_identifier`, or `replicate_source_db` is provided). Password for the master DB user. Note that this may show up in logs, and it will be stored in the state file
db_instance.name: password_wo

db_instance.description: The port on which the DB accepts connections
db_instance.name: port

db_instance.description: Specifies whether or not to create this database from a snapshot. This correlates to the snapshot ID you'd find in the RDS console, e.g: rds:production-2015-06-26-06-05.
db_instance.name: snapshot_identifier

db_instance.description: Description of the monitoring IAM role
db_instance.name: monitoring_role_description

db_instance.description: Create IAM role with a defined name that permits RDS to send enhanced monitoring metrics to CloudWatch Logs.
db_instance.name: create_monitoring_role

db_instance.description: The national character set is used in the NCHAR, NVARCHAR2, and NCLOB data types for Oracle instances. This can't be changed.
db_instance.name: nchar_character_set_name

db_instance.description: Specifies whether Performance Insights are enabled
db_instance.name: performance_insights_enabled

db_instance.description: Determines whether to use `monitoring_role_name` as is or create a unique identifier beginning with `monitoring_role_name` as the specified prefix
db_instance.name: monitoring_role_use_name_prefix

db_instance.description: The instance type of the RDS instance
db_instance.name: instance_class

db_instance.description: Username for the master DB user
db_instance.name: username

db_instance.description: Indicates that major version upgrades are allowed. Changing this parameter does not result in an outage and the change is asynchronously applied as soon as possible
db_instance.name: allow_major_version_upgrade

db_instance.description: A mapping of tags to assign to all resources
db_instance.name: tags

db_instance.description: The amount of time in days to retain Performance Insights data. Either 7 (7 days) or 731 (2 years).
db_instance.name: performance_insights_retention_period

db_instance.description: Determines whether a CloudWatch log group is created for each `enabled_cloudwatch_logs_exports`
db_instance.name: create_cloudwatch_log_group

db_instance.description: A cron() or rate() expression that defines the schedule for rotating your secret. Either automatically_after_days or schedule_expression must be specified.
db_instance.name: master_user_password_rotation_schedule_expression

db_instance.description: Determines whether to use `identifier` as is or create a unique identifier beginning with `identifier` as the specified prefix
db_instance.name: use_identifier_prefix

db_instance.description: The database engine to use
db_instance.name: engine

db_instance.description: Name of the DB option group to associate.
db_instance.name: option_group_name

db_instance.description: Time zone of the DB instance. timezone is currently only supported by Microsoft SQL Server. The timezone can only be set on creation. See MSSQL User Guide for more information.
db_instance.name: timezone

db_instance.description: Updated Terraform resource management timeouts. Applies to `aws_db_instance` in particular to permit resource management times
db_instance.name: timeouts

db_instance.description: Specifies the number of days between automatic scheduled rotations of the secret. Either automatically_after_days or schedule_expression must be specified.
db_instance.name: master_user_password_rotation_automatically_after_days

db_instance.description: The name of the RDS instance
db_instance.name: identifier

db_instance.description: The ID of the Directory Service Active Directory domain to create the instance in
db_instance.name: domain

db_instance.description: (Optional, but required if domain_fqdn is provided) The IPv4 DNS IP addresses of your primary and secondary self managed Active Directory domain controllers. Two IP addresses must be provided. If there isn't a secondary domain controller, use the IP address of the primary domain controller for both entries in the list. Conflicts with domain and domain_iam_role_name.
db_instance.name: domain_dns_ips

db_instance.description: The name which is prefixed to the final snapshot on cluster destroy
db_instance.name: final_snapshot_identifier_prefix

db_instance.description: Indicates whether to enable a customer-owned IP address (CoIP) for an RDS on Outposts DB instance
db_instance.name: customer_owned_ip_enabled

db_instance.description: The ARN for the IAM role that permits RDS to send enhanced monitoring metrics to CloudWatch Logs. Must be specified if monitoring_interval is non-zero.
db_instance.name: monitoring_role_arn

db_instance.description: The daily time range (in UTC) during which automated backups are created if they are enabled. Example: '09:46-10:16'. Must not overlap with maintenance_window
db_instance.name: backup_window

db_instance.description: Specifies the identifier of the CA certificate for the DB instance
db_instance.name: ca_cert_identifier

root.description: Create IAM role with a defined name that permits RDS to send enhanced monitoring metrics to CloudWatch Logs
root.name: create_monitoring_role

root.description: The daily time range (in UTC) during which automated backups are created if they are enabled. Example: '09:46-10:16'. Must not overlap with maintenance_window
root.name: backup_window

root.description: A list of DB parameters (map) to apply
root.name: parameters

root.description: A list of Options to apply
root.name: options

root.description: (Required if domain is provided) The name of the IAM role to be used when making API calls to the Directory Service
root.name: domain_iam_role_name

root.description:
The key ARN, key ID, alias ARN or alias name for the KMS key to encrypt the master user password secret in Secrets Manager.
  If not specified, the default KMS key for your Amazon Web Services account is used.
root.name: master_user_secret_kms_key_id

root.description: The port on which the DB accepts connections
root.name: port

root.description: The mode of Database Insights that is enabled for the instance. Valid values: standard, advanced
root.name: database_insights_mode

root.description: Indicates that minor engine upgrades will be applied automatically to the DB instance during the maintenance window
root.name: auto_minor_version_upgrade

root.description: Additional tags for the DB option group
root.name: db_option_group_tags

root.description: Additional tags for the DB subnet group
root.name: db_subnet_group_tags

root.description: Determines whether a CloudWatch log group is created for each `enabled_cloudwatch_logs_exports`
root.name: create_cloudwatch_log_group

root.description: The DB name to create. If omitted, no database is created initially
root.name: db_name

root.description: ARN of the policy that is used to set the permissions boundary for the monitoring IAM role
root.name: monitoring_role_permissions_boundary

root.description: Use a dedicated log volume (DLV) for the DB instance. Requires Provisioned IOPS.
root.name: dedicated_log_volume

root.description: Name of the DB parameter group to associate or create
root.name: parameter_group_name

root.description: The character set name to use for DB encoding in Oracle instances. This can't be changed. See Oracle Character Sets Supported in Amazon RDS and Collations and Character Sets for Microsoft SQL Server for more information. This can only be set on creation
root.name: character_set_name

root.description: The ARN for the KMS key to encrypt Performance Insights data
root.name: performance_insights_kms_key_id

root.description: Set to true if you do not wish the log group (and any logs it may contain) to be deleted at destroy time, and instead just remove the log group from the Terraform state
root.name: cloudwatch_log_group_skip_destroy

root.description: The database engine to use
root.name: engine

root.description: The instance type of the RDS instance
root.name: instance_class

root.description: Used together with password_wo to trigger an update. Increment this value when an update to password_wo is required.
root.name: password_wo_version

root.description: The Availability Zone of the RDS instance
root.name: availability_zone

root.description: Name of the IAM role which will be created when create_monitoring_role is enabled
root.name: monitoring_role_name

root.description: Restore from a Percona Xtrabackup in S3 (only MySQL is supported)
root.name: s3_import

root.description: The description of the option group
root.name: option_group_description

root.description: The days to retain backups for
root.name: backup_retention_period

root.description: Additional tags for the DB instance
root.name: db_instance_tags

root.description: Determines whether to use `subnet_group_name` as is or create a unique name beginning with the `subnet_group_name` as the prefix
root.name: db_subnet_group_use_name_prefix

root.description: Description of the DB subnet group to create
root.name: db_subnet_group_description

root.description: Time zone of the DB instance. timezone is currently only supported by Microsoft SQL Server. The timezone can only be set on creation. See MSSQL User Guide for more information
root.name: timezone

root.description: The ID of the Directory Service Active Directory domain to create the instance in
root.name: domain

root.description: Indicates whether to enable a customer-owned IP address (CoIP) for an RDS on Outposts DB instance
root.name: customer_owned_ip_enabled

root.description: Bool to control if instance is publicly accessible
root.name: publicly_accessible

root.description: The national character set is used in the NCHAR, NVARCHAR2, and NCLOB data types for Oracle instances. This can't be changed.
root.name: nchar_character_set_name

root.description: The number of days to retain CloudWatch logs for the DB instance
root.name: cloudwatch_log_group_retention_in_days

root.description: A map of DB instance supported feature name to role association ARNs.
root.name: db_instance_role_associations

root.description: Specifies the number of days between automatic scheduled rotations of the secret. Either automatically_after_days or schedule_expression must be specified.
root.name: master_user_password_rotation_automatically_after_days

root.description: Set to true to allow RDS to manage the master user password in Secrets Manager
root.name: manage_master_user_password

root.description: The ARN for the IAM role that permits RDS to send enhanced monitoring metrics to CloudWatch Logs. Must be specified if monitoring_interval is non-zero
root.name: monitoring_role_arn

root.description: Specifies the major version of the engine that this option group should be associated with
root.name: major_engine_version

root.description: Specifies whether Performance Insights are enabled
root.name: performance_insights_enabled

root.description: The ARN of the KMS Key to use when encrypting log data
root.name: cloudwatch_log_group_kms_key_id

root.description: The ARN for the KMS encryption key. If creating an encrypted replica, set this to the destination KMS ARN. If storage_encrypted is set to true and kms_key_id is not specified the default KMS key created in your account will be used. Be sure to use the full ARN, not a key alias.
root.name: kms_key_id

root.description: (Optional, but required if domain_fqdn is provided) The IPv4 DNS IP addresses of your primary and secondary self managed Active Directory domain controllers. Two IP addresses must be provided. If there isn't a secondary domain controller, use the IP address of the primary domain controller for both entries in the list. Conflicts with domain and domain_iam_role_name.
root.name: domain_dns_ips

root.description: Specifies whether any database modifications are applied immediately, or during the next maintenance window
root.name: apply_immediately

root.description: Set to true if you do not wish the option group to be deleted at destroy time, and instead just remove the option group from the Terraform state
root.name: option_group_skip_destroy

root.description: Additional tags for the CloudWatch log group(s)
root.name: cloudwatch_log_group_tags

root.description: The length of the rotation window in hours. For example, 3h for a three hour window.
root.name: master_user_password_rotation_duration

root.description: Restore to a point in time (MySQL is NOT supported)
root.name: restore_to_point_in_time
root.type:
object({
    restore_time                             = optional(string)
    source_db_instance_automated_backups_arn = optional(string)
    source_db_instance_identifier            = optional(string)
    source_dbi_resource_id                   = optional(string)
    use_latest_restorable_time               = optional(bool)
  })

root.description: Specifies whether or not the mappings of AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) accounts to database accounts are enabled
root.name: iam_database_authentication_enabled

root.description: Determines whether to use `monitoring_role_name` as is or create a unique identifier beginning with `monitoring_role_name` as the specified prefix
root.name: monitoring_role_use_name_prefix

root.description: Additional tags for the  DB parameter group
root.name: db_parameter_group_tags

root.description: Whether to create a database parameter group
root.name: create_db_parameter_group

root.description: The family of the DB parameter group
root.name: family

root.description: The amount of time in days to retain Performance Insights data. Valid values are `7`, `731` (2 years) or a multiple of `31`
root.name: performance_insights_retention_period

db_instance.description: The availability zone of the RDS instance
db_instance.name: db_instance_availability_zone

db_instance.description: The connection endpoint
db_instance.name: db_instance_endpoint

db_instance.description: Specifies the listener connection endpoint for SQL Server Always On
db_instance.name: db_listener_endpoint

db_instance.description: The canonical hosted zone ID of the DB instance (to be used in a Route 53 Alias record)
db_instance.name: db_instance_hosted_zone_id

db_instance.description: The RDS Resource ID of this instance
db_instance.name: db_instance_resource_id

db_instance.description: The database name
db_instance.name: db_instance_name

db_instance.description: Specifies the identifier of the CA certificate for the DB instance
db_instance.name: db_instance_ca_cert_identifier

db_instance.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) specifying the monitoring role
db_instance.name: enhanced_monitoring_iam_role_arn

db_instance.description: The ARN of the RDS instance
db_instance.name: db_instance_arn

db_instance.description: The RDS instance identifier
db_instance.name: db_instance_identifier

db_instance.description: The IPv4 DNS IP addresses of your primary and secondary self managed Active Directory domain controllers
db_instance.name: db_instance_domain_dns_ips

db_instance.description: Order in which the instances are upgraded (first, second, last)
db_instance.name: db_instance_upgrade_rollout_order

db_instance.description: The address of the RDS instance
db_instance.name: db_instance_address

db_instance.description: The database engine
db_instance.name: db_instance_engine

db_instance.description: The master username for the database
db_instance.name: db_instance_username

db_instance.description: The ARN for the Secrets Manager secret with the self managed Active Directory credentials for the user joining the domain
db_instance.name: db_instance_domain_auth_secret_arn

db_instance.description: The self managed Active Directory organizational unit for your DB instance to join
db_instance.name: db_instance_domain_ou

db_instance.description: The ARN of the master user secret (Only available when manage_master_user_password is set to true)
db_instance.name: db_instance_master_user_secret_arn

db_instance.description: Map of CloudWatch log groups created and their attributes
db_instance.name: db_instance_cloudwatch_log_groups

db_instance.description: Specifies whether automatic rotation is enabled for the secret
db_instance.name: db_instance_secretsmanager_secret_rotation_enabled

db_instance.description: The name of the monitoring role
db_instance.name: enhanced_monitoring_iam_role_name

db_instance.description: The running version of the database
db_instance.name: db_instance_engine_version_actual

db_instance.description: The RDS instance status
db_instance.name: db_instance_status

db_instance.description: The database port
db_instance.name: db_instance_port

db_instance.description: The ID of the Directory Service Active Directory domain the instance is joined to
db_instance.name: db_instance_domain

db_instance.description: The fully qualified domain name (FQDN) of an self managed Active Directory domain
db_instance.name: db_instance_domain_fqdn

db_instance.description: The name of the IAM role to be used when making API calls to the Directory Service
db_instance.name: db_instance_domain_iam_role_name

db_instance_role_association.description: DB Instance Identifier and IAM Role ARN separated by a comma
db_instance_role_association.name: db_instance_role_association_id

db_option_group.description: The ARN of the db option group
db_option_group.name: db_option_group_arn

db_parameter_group.description: The ARN of the db parameter group
db_parameter_group.name: db_parameter_group_arn

db_subnet_group.name: db_subnet_group_arn
